taxonomy: Caspareopsis purpurea is classified in the family Sapindaceae and is native to tropical regions. It is a type of tree that belongs to the order Sapindales. The classification of Caspareopsis purpurea is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida. The order Sapindales is a group of plants that includes other species such as raintree and lychee. The classification of Caspareopsis purpurea is a way to understand its evolutionary relationships and characteristics.
uses: Caspareopsis purpurea has been traditionally used in folk medicine in the Caribbean for its purported medicinal properties.
